inlineContent.text: These two hands-on tutorials take you from a basic data source to a fully reactive, dependency-driven data layer. By the end, you will know how to handle caching, retry, auto-refresh, and upstream state changes in real SwiftUI apps.

sections.title: Build Reactive Data Layers with Silo

inlineContent.text: Build a D6 dice roller from scratch, learn the core
lifecycle, how to drive a SwiftUI view from a reactive stream, and how to configure a source to refresh itself automatically while it has active subscribers.
Build a configurable dice roller that re-rolls automatically when the player changes the dice type or count, learn the difference between
prevents stale results, and how to combine multiple dependency streams.

chapter-dice-sides.svg.alt: Dice icon representing the reactive dependencies tutorial

chapter-d6.svg.alt: Dice icon representing the D6 dice roller tutorial

abstract.text: In this tutorial, you will build a D6 dice roller using Silo. The fetch is a single line —
— so every concept you encounter is focused on Silo, with no boilerplate distractions.

Build a configurable dice roller that automatically re-rolls whenever the player changes the dice type or count. You will learn how to declare dependencies, choose the right refresh policy, and wire SwiftUI pickers to reactive streams — resulting in a fully functional application.
